caption:
Follow (us) @thefunniestbloopers for more! 🎬🍿

🎬 Curb Your Enthusiasm | 2000–2024 · Comedy · 30m

📜 Larry David plays an over-the-top version of himself, navigating the social minefields of Los Angeles. Every episode follows Larry as he gets into awkward confrontations over unwritten social rules, small annoyances, and his own stubborn refusal to let things go.

👐 A pioneer of cringe comedy, the show is famous for being almost entirely improvised based on detailed story outlines rather than traditional scripts.

📅 U.S. theatrical release: October 15, 2000 (Series Premiere)
🎬 Director: Various (Including Robert B. Weide and Jeff Schaffer)
✍️ Writers: Larry David
🎭 Cast: Larry David (Himself), Jeff Garlin (Jeff Greene), Cheryl Hines (Cheryl David), Susie Essman (Susie Greene), J.B. Smoove (Leon Black)
💰 Budget: N/A
💵 Worldwide gross: N/A
🏢 Distributor: HBO
⏱️ Runtime: 30–60 Minutes
⭐ IMDb rating: 8.8/10
🍅 Rotten Tomatoes critics score: 92%

💡 Did you know? In a bizarre twist of fate, unused footage from an episode filmed at a Dodgers game actually provided an alibi for a man named Juan Catalan, clearing him of a murder charge by proving he was at the stadium.
summary: Overview of Curb Your Enthusiasm, HBO's acclaimed cringe comedy series featuring Larry David navigating social awkwardness through largely improvised episodes.

caption:
Oaxaca is easily one of the best food cities I've been to and that's just based on a few hours of grazing. Tlayudas from Las Tlayudas de Mina y Bustamante. Salsa molcajete made with roasted Chiles de agua and tomatoes, a blue corn tostada, and a quesadilla with mushrooms and a sheath of crisp fried shredded pork fat from Casa Oaxaca. Random tacos de consommé de res from the street. Properly frothy champurrado.

We took a zapoteca cooking class in Teotitlan this morning and are eating at Origen tonight. Loving everything so far.
summary: Kenji López-Alt shares highlights from a food-focused trip to Oaxaca, including tlayudas, salsa, quesadillas, and street tacos from local vendors.

caption:
I've lost a lot of weight over the past few years. My grandma, who doesn't have the sharpest of memories, is shocked and thrilled everytime she sees me, remembering only "fat" me of the past, forgetting we've gone through all of the weight comments on every single visit since this weight loss journey began. Last visit, she looked at me, beaming, and said, "You look so good! Who would have thought.... underneath all that fat was ✨THIS!✨" 

It's strange figuring out how to react to comments like this. She means well and it's really not worth getting into why comments like this aren't actually beneficial. Grandma has gained and lost weight her entire adult life and punishes herself when she's on the heavier side. She's now the thinnest she's ever been, a situation she celebrates, and we have to keep politely reminding her that at 90, being very small isn't always a good thing and that maybe gaining a little weight would be beneficial. It is insane to me that the THIN IS GOOD mantra is so deeply ingrained that she's still prioritizing it at this stage in life. 

Deconditioning is a process. We all go through it. And when the judgmental voices make their way into your thoughts, if the person the thoughts are about isn't hurting anyone, "do you, boo" truly is the best mantra. 
Credit/ dinosaurs-and-philions & nikolaecuza & quadzilla-rising
summary: A reflection on weight loss, family comments about appearance, and how societal beauty standards affect people across generations.

caption:
Wait… you're working out, but not trying to lose weight? 🤯

If you've ever told someone you're just moving your body to feel good, and they stared at you like you just spoke an alien language... you're not alone. 👀

We've been so conditioned to think that every workout has to be about shrinking ourselves or chasing some aesthetic goal. And there's absolutely nothing wrong with aesthetic goals—those are valid!

But once I stopped working out only to hit a number on the scale or look a certain way, something clicked. 💡 I started showing up more consistently, felt less pressure, and realized how much better I feel when I move my body. Less guilt, more joy.

It's hard for some to wrap their heads around the idea that we can work out just for us—for how it makes us feel, not just for how it makes us look. But here's your reminder: you're allowed to work out because you enjoy it, because it makes you stronger, clears your mind, or just puts you in a better mood. That's more than enough. 💪✨

Keep doing what feels right for YOU.
summary: A reflection on working out for mental and physical well-being rather than solely for aesthetic or weight-loss goals.

caption: SAN MATEO, CA—After spending the past three decades of his life being totally unable and unwilling to engage in any meaningful way with the world around him, James Parker, a local guy who sucks at being a person, told reporters Thursday that he saw huge potential in AI. "While it's still in its early phase, artificial intelligence will one day accomplish things that humans could have never even dreamed of doing," said Parker, who, by all accounts, has never stretched himself to do something he found difficult; has never created anything truly original; and, deep down, has absolutely zero understanding of what makes things good, enjoyable, or rewarding.
summary: The Onion satirizes a man with no personal accomplishments expressing enthusiasm about AI's potential.

caption:
I'LL DO IT LATER: A French Quarter woman who was nice enough to take photos of a girls outing has been accused of holding the images hostage this week, as her mates report that she still hasn't uploaded them to the group chat, despite the outing occurring a week ago.,

Vanessa Aires, 27 is alleged to have responded multiple times that she will upload the photos 'soon' or 'when she gets home', despite the simple action only requiring thirty seconds of effort.

Her friends speak to The Advocate to vent their frustrations.

"We ended up going to Skii bar, which overlooks the entire city", explains Sheevaun, Vanessa's best work friend, "so we thought it'd be nice to take some photos in front of the sunset."

"I think we forced the poor bartender to take 100 of them."

Sheevaun says she'd immediately asked Vanessa to upload the photos into the group chat, but was assured they'd all be uploaded later.

"I've asked so many fucking times, I swear at this point, it must be some weird power struggle."

Vanessa's friend Tara concludes that she has also tried to get these photos, to no success.

"I've asked her several times and she's just stopped responding."

Reaching out to Vanessa, our reporter is quickly able to discern that she does, in fact, seem to get some sick thrill from it.

"Haha yeah I knowww, I'll get around to it haha", says Vanessa, insincerely, "I've just been so busy."

More to come.
summary: A satirical news article about a woman who refuses to upload photos from a girls' night out despite repeated requests from her frustrated friends.

caption:
I'm in SF now through lunch. Anywhere great and low key I should check out for lunch in North Beach or the Mission (or anywhere en route between?

Doesn't have to be new, just good and easy. (Yes my favorite taco truck anywhere @elgallogirotruck) is already in the plans.)

I'm also excited to re-visit my old restaurant @wursthall in San Mateo this evening. It'll be the first time in over a year and it's always nice to see how everyone is doing. From all accounts the kitchen team is stronger than it's ever been.

Also also looking forward to brunch tomorrow at @backhausbread's new Burlingame cafe location which opened about a month ago.* The menu looks great and I already know you won't find better bread and pastries anywhere. 

Disclosure: I have no ongoing business/financial relationship with Wursthall and have not since late 2020. I am a partner at Backhaus but have zero to do with their day to day operations. The excellent bread and pastry is 100% the work of Anne and her team.
summary: Kenji López-Alt seeks lunch recommendations in SF and shares plans to visit favorite restaurants including El Gallo Giro taco truck, Wursthall, and Backhaus Bread's new Burlingame location.

caption:
Pre leg day stretches / mobility 🌶️ @weglow 

Save and add to your routine! 🤝🏼

I do each for about 30-45 seconds to feel nice and warmed up! Mobility routines don't have to be hard or complicated as long as you're focusing on the muscles you're training - you're good! Stay consistent with warming up your body before your workouts to prevent injury and focus on longevity! Something I'm making more of a priority in my routine. 😬

For more of my workouts, nutrition, and guides - train with me on @weglow through the link in my bio. 🫶🏼

#WeGLOW #ad

#legdaymobility #legdaymobilityroutine #mobility #mobilitytraining #mobilityexercises #mobilitywork #legdaystretch #legdaystaples #lowerbodyroutine #lowerbodymobility #fitness #mobilitydrills

caption:
𝕋𝕙𝕖 𝔼𝕒𝕤𝕚𝕖𝕤𝕥 ℕ𝕠-𝕂𝕟𝕖𝕒𝕕 𝔽𝕠𝕔𝕒𝕔𝕔𝕚𝕒 𝕄𝕦𝕗𝕗𝕚𝕟 ♡

⭐️ᴘʟᴇᴀsᴇ ғɪɴᴅ ɴᴏɴ-ᴍᴇᴛʀɪᴄ ʀᴇᴄɪᴘᴇ ɪɴ ᴘɪɴɴᴇᴅ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴇɴᴛ 

⭐️ ᴘʟᴇᴀsᴇ ғɪɴᴅ sᴏᴜʀᴅᴏᴜɢʜ ʀᴇᴄɪᴘᴇ ɪɴ ᴘɪɴɴᴇᴅ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴇɴᴛ 

🅡🅔🅒🅘🅟🅔
(Makes 12)

350g Bread Flour
245g Water
*2g Instant Yeast
7g Salt
10g Olive oil

*Substitutable with 70g Levain/ Discard👌

Toppings 

Smoked Sea Salt
Rosemary

🅜🅔🅣🅗🅞🅓

ғɪʀsᴛ ᴘʀᴏᴏғ

➡️ Mix flour, oil, salt, instant yeast and water and let it sit for 30min.
	
➡️ 1 set of stretch-and fold, rest 30 min.

➡️ 2 sets coil fold, 30min rest in-between. 

➡️ Overnight cold fermentation in the refrigerator.

sᴇᴄᴏɴᴅ ᴘʀᴏᴏғ

➡️ The next day, divide the dough into 12 equal portions.

➡️ Roll each portion into a ball and place in muffin tray.

➡️ Proof until (almost) double. Preheat oven at 230C. 

➡️ Dimple, add desired toppings and bake for 18-20min. 

Bon Appetite!

.
.
.
.
.
.

#Sourdough#homebaking#homemade#levitomadre#sourdoughfocaccia#bread#bake#artisanbread#explorepage#slime#miniature#recipes#homecooking#recipereel#naturalfermentation#asmr#satisfying#minifocaccia#bakersofinstagram#levain#shebakesourdough#focaccia#sourdoughstarter#sweetstiffstarter#bakingtipsandtricks#masamadre#天然酵母#天然酵母パン
summary: A no-knead sourdough focaccia muffin recipe with rosemary and sea salt toppings.

caption:
I am not the kind of person to buy into hype or place too much trust on "best of" lists, but the fried chicken at @thechickensupply in Phinney Ridge tests the limits of how good and crispy fried chicken can be. It's incredible. Mind-blowingly good. I didn't know chicken can do this, and we haven't even tried the sides yet. (Also: everything is just gluten free by default).

Thanks to my friend Oliver who took the initiative to place an order before they sold out at 4:15 today. (They sell out every day). 

Also a huge thank you to the crew there who were obviously at the tail end of a really busy day but were still exceedingly friendly and accommodating (also I didn't catch who it was, but whoever was singing from the kitchen, you've got the voice of an angel and should win karaoke competitions.)
summary: Kenji López-Alt raves about exceptional fried chicken at The Chicken Supply in Seattle, praising its crispiness and quality.

caption: Have an e-mail you've been meaning (but not really) to reply to? Swipe through for some reasons your response has been delayed, and find more valid excuses at the link in our bio.
summary: A New Yorker cartoon offering humorous excuses for delayed email replies.

caption:
How to pick good gelato from bad when you're in Italy from a Roman local herself, Val!!! Honestly, even the worst gelato we had was considerably decent BUT following these tips definitely led us to our favorite gelato spots! We loved @gelateriafatamorgana and @gunthergelatoroma 😍

A lot of y'all asked what tour this was — this was the Rome Twilight Tastevere Food Tour with @eatingeurope and we booked through @viatortravel (not guaranteed to get Val as your guide) *not sponsored

UPDATE: Val has created a public IG page! You can show your love and support on her page @valentina.petraroli ❤️
summary: A Roman local shares tips for identifying quality gelato in Italy and recommends favorite gelato shops.
